steps.title: Multiply both sides by v squared to clear the denominator
steps.content: Starting with $47=\frac{2K}{v^{2}}$, multiply both sides by $v^2$: $$47v^2 = 2K$$

steps.title: Divide by 47 and take the square root
steps.content: Divide both sides by 47 to isolate $v^2$: $$v^2 = \frac{2K}{47}$$ Since $v > 0$, take the positive square root of both sides: $$v = \sqrt{\frac{2K}{47}}$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
